steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes a type of feature found in live performances — features that "result from impromptu deviations of performers when fidelity to an established musical score is not mandated." The word impromptu (spontaneous, unplanned) is the decisive clue: the blank must mean the same thing.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — inevitable: inevitable means certain to happen regardless of circumstances. Deviations from a score are not unavoidable by nature; they happen when a performer chooses to deviate. This describes certainty, not spontaneity. ✗
  • Choice B — inconspicuous: inconspicuous means not easily noticed. This describes how visible the features are, not how they arise. The passage is about the origin of the features (impromptu deviation), not their visibility. ✗
  • Choice C — extemporaneous: extemporaneous means done without preparation, improvised on the spot. This is a direct synonym for impromptu and correctly names features that arise from unplanned, in-the-moment deviations. ✓
  • Choice D — meticulous: meticulous means extremely careful and precise. This is the opposite of impromptu — a meticulous performer would follow the score exactly rather than deviate from it. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
